title: Entry Deduplication & Signal Re-scan Audit

production_universe_dataset_impact.note: Only internal_paper_trades-sourced dataset rows are candidates for clustering overlap; historical_outcomes rows excluded from this check by definition.

verdict_reason: 24.72% of CP-039D dataset rows are excess duplicate-cluster members (re-scan artifacts at ~20s intervals). This partially explains WF instability in CP-038D/CP-040A and should be addressed via dedup before further model training, but does not invalidate the entire dataset.
notes: CP-045 is READ-ONLY. No runtime, execution, or DB changes made.
No model retraining or promotion performed.
Recommendation: design dedup/cooldown logic before next ML training cycle (out of scope for this audit).
verdict_basis: Corrected: based on dataset-level overlap (Section C), not raw internal_paper_trades-wide clustering rate.
superseded_note: Initial automated verdict (FAIL, based on 87.08% raw table-wide clustering) was overly broad. Corrected verdict uses the actual CP-039D dataset overlap (24.72% excess), which is the metric relevant to ML training integrity.
